title: Triple-Hormone-Receptor Agonist Retatrutide for Obesity — A Phase 2 Trial
population: 338 adults with obesity or overweight and a weight-related condition, without diabetes
intervention: once-weekly retatrutide 1 mg, 4 mg, 8 mg or 12 mg with differing starting doses
key_results: At 48 weeks, mean bodyweight change at 12 mg was approximately -24.2% versus -2.1% with placebo.
Weight reduction had not clearly plateaued at the end of the trial.
safety_findings: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common and were dose related.
Dose-dependent increases in heart rate were observed and later declined after peaking.
limitations: Phase 2 size and duration; not designed to establish long-term cardiovascular outcomes or regulatory approval.
investigational_status_notice: Retatrutide remains investigational and is not approved for public use.
